CCR.Cause: Stopcock position not detected after confirming proper setup and replacing pump set.
CCR.Resolution: Re-primed system, verified setup, replaced feeding set, and removed pump from service when error persisted.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 21 Cannot find stopcock feed position during priming
description: Error 21 during priming prevents detection of stopcock feed position, stopping the pump and interrupting enteral feeding setup.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop the pump, disconnect from patient if needed, and transition to a backup feeding device.

steps.title: Verify Error Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 21 appears during priming and observe timing and pump response.

steps.title: Check Pump Set Installation
steps.instructions: Reseat feeding set, align tubing correctly, and ensure cassette and door are properly secured.

steps.title: Inspect Feeding Set
steps.instructions: Verify correct set type and replace if damaged or defective.

steps.title: Re-Prime System
steps.instructions: Restart the pump and initiate priming to confirm proper function.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing and cassette area for kinks, pressure, or resistance affecting movement.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 21 during priming and unable to initiate feeding.
CCR.Cause: Error persisted after replacing feeding set and verifying setup, indicating possible internal sensing failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent for repair after external causes were ruled out.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 22 Motor error during tubing auto-load
description: Error 22 during auto-load prevents tubing from seating properly due to motor or mechanical engagement failure, stopping feeding from starting.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from use and transition to a backup feeding method.

steps.title: Verify Error Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 22 appears during tubing auto-load and observe motor engagement failure.

steps.title: Reload Pump Set
steps.instructions: Remove and properly reinstall tubing ensuring correct routing and seating.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, damage, or deformation and replace if necessary.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect rotor path for debris or dried formula preventing movement.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Restart the device to clear temporary motor or control faults.

steps.title: Test with New Pump Set
steps.instructions: Use a new feeding set to rule out accessory-related issues.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 22 during tubing auto-load; unable to initiate feeding.
CCR.Cause: Motor error during auto-load. Reload and tubing replacement did not resolve issue, indicating internal failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 23 Motor failsafe timeout (possible encoder/flex cable issue)
description: Error 23 indicates a motor failsafe timeout, stopping feeding due to loss of motor or encoder feedback.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop pump use immediately and transition to a backup feeding device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 23 appears and observe when it occurs during operation.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn off, disconnect power, wait, and restart the pump.

steps.title: Check Pump Set Installation
steps.instructions: Reinstall tubing and ensure proper seating in the rotor.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Issues
steps.instructions: Check for resistance, noise, or signs of damage.

steps.title: Test Without Load
steps.instructions: Run the pump without tubing to isolate internal faults.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 23 and not delivering feed.
CCR.Cause: Motor failsafe timeout due to loss of encoder or motor feedback.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair evaluation.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 33 Motor control failure
description: Error 33 indicates a motor control failure causing the feeding pump to stop or fail to deliver enteral nutrition.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop feeding immediately, remove the pump from patient use, and transfer to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify the Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 33 is displayed and observe motor behavior and noise during operation.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Turn off, disconnect power, remove battery if possible, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify connection to a working outlet and confirm stable power or battery function.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Mechanism
steps.instructions: Check rotor and tubing channel for debris or obstruction; ensure free movement.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Set
steps.instructions: Install a new feeding set and attempt normal operation.

steps.title: Observe Motor Function
steps.instructions: Run pump off patient and evaluate if motor engages, stalls, or fails to move.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 33 and not delivering feeding.
CCR.Cause: Motor control failure confirmed after external checks and power cycle, indicating internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 50 Graphic display timeout (status check failure)
description: Error 50 indicates a display timeout causing screen freeze, loss of interface control, or inability to safely operate the feeding pump.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop using the pump and transfer feeding to a backup device to maintain safe patient care.

steps.title: Verify the Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 50 appears and observe display behavior including freeze, blank screen, or partial response.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Turn off, disconnect power, remove battery if possible, wait, then restart to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions: Use a known-good outlet and verify stable AC or battery operation.

steps.title: Inspect Display Condition
steps.instructions: Check for physical damage, liquid intrusion, or lack of button response.

steps.title: Test Basic Functionality
steps.instructions: Attempt button inputs and listen for responses to determine if issue is display-only or system-wide.

steps.title: Escalate if Unresolved
steps.instructions: Remove from service and send for repair if issue persists after all external checks.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 50 with frozen or unresponsive screen during use.
CCR.Cause: Display timeout persists after reset indicating internal display or control board failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair evaluation.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 51 Graphic Display Timeout (Auto Mode Data Write Failure)
description: Error 51 indicates a display communication failure causing pump interruption, frozen screen, or inability to operate safely.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop pump use and transfer feeding to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 51 is displayed and observe device behavior.

steps.title: Power Cycle Device
steps.instructions: Turn off, remove power and battery, then restart pump.

steps.title: Check Power Stability
steps.instructions: Inspect outlet, cord, and battery seating.

steps.title: Inspect Device Condition
steps.instructions: Check for physical damage or display issues.

steps.title: Test Different Modes
steps.instructions: Attempt operation in different modes to isolate failure.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 51 with frozen or unresponsive display.
CCR.Cause: Internal communication failure between display and control board after external checks.
CCR.Resolution: Power cycled with no resolution; removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo ePump Feeding Pump - Error: 52 Graphic display timeout (write bytes failure)
description: Error 52 indicates a display communication failure causing frozen or unresponsive screen and inability to safely operate the feeding pump.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop feeding and move patient to a backup pump to maintain safe nutrition delivery.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 52 appears and observe if the display is frozen, blank, or partially functional.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn off the pump, disconnect power, remove battery, wait, then restart to reset communication.

steps.title: Check Power Stability
steps.instructions: Use a known-good outlet and ensure battery is properly seated to rule out power-related issues.

steps.title: Inspect for Damage
steps.instructions: Check for cracks, fluid intrusion, or physical damage around the display and housing.

steps.title: Test Basic Functionality
steps.instructions: Attempt to navigate menus and verify if controls respond despite display issues.

steps.title: Remove from Service if Unresolved
steps.instructions: If error persists, take device out of service and send for repair.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 52 with frozen or unresponsive screen during feeding.
CCR.Cause: Display/control board communication failure not resolved by power cycle.
CCR.Resolution: Performed power cycle and inspection; issue persisted. Removed device from service and sent for repair.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Pump Inactive
description: Troubleshooting the Pump Inactive alarm on the Kangaroo OMNI when the pump has been without user input for more than 10 minutes, including patient safety checks, workflow verification, Continue button response, and escalation if controls do not respond.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is connected to a patient and feeding or flushing may have been interrupted, notify clinical staff before clearing or changing anything. Do not assume therapy is continuing just because the pump is powered on.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact message shown is Pump Inactive. Ask whether the pump was being programmed, paused, left on a setup screen, or awaiting confirmation before the alarm appeared.

steps.title: Confirm Whether the Pump Was Left Without Input
steps.instructions: Determine whether the Kangaroo OMNI was left without user input for more than 10 minutes on a programming, setup, pause, or confirmation screen.

steps.title: Press Continue
steps.instructions: Press Continue on the pump screen and verify that the pump returns to the previous screen. Confirm the pump status with clinical staff before returning it to use.

steps.title: Verify the Pump Is Actually Running if Feeding Is Needed
steps.instructions: After clearing the alarm, confirm whether the pump is running, paused, awaiting programming, waiting for confirmation, or not delivering. Clearing the alarm does not automatically mean feeding has resumed.

steps.title: Check for Repeated Inactivity Complaints
steps.instructions: If the alarm repeats, ask whether the pump is often being programmed and left unattended before Start or Run is selected. Determine whether the issue is workflow-related.

steps.title: Inspect the Touchscreen and Controls if the Alarm Will Not Clear
steps.instructions: If Continue does not respond, inspect for cracked screen, delayed touch response, no touch response, fluid contamination, damaged buttons, or debris interfering with input.

steps.title: Power Cycle Only if Clinically Safe
steps.instructions: If the screen is frozen or controls do not respond, remove the pump from patient use first. Power cycle according to facility practice and verify normal restart and input response.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If Continue clears the alarm, controls respond normally, and the pump can be programmed and started as expected, return the pump to service and document the finding as an inactivity timeout.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI reported with Pump Inactive alarm. Staff stated pump displayed inactivity screen after being left without input during setup.
CCR.Cause: Pump Inactive alarm verified. Alarm occurred because the pump had no user input for more than 10 minutes. Continue button responded normally and no hardware failure was found.
CCR.Resolution: Pressed Continue and pump returned to previous screen. Verified display and controls responded normally. Pump programmed and function checked successfully. Returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Low Battery Notice
description: Troubleshooting Low Battery Notice alarms on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump when approximately 30 minutes of battery life remains and therapy reliability may be affected.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm with clinical staff before interrupting feeding or flushing. Restore power before the battery is depleted and move the patient to a backup pump if therapy reliability is uncertain.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the alarm displayed is Low Battery Notice. Check whether it occurs on battery, during feeding or flushing, immediately after unplugging, or while connected to wall power.

steps.title: Connect the Pump to Wall Power
steps.instructions: Plug the Kangaroo OMNI into a known-good wall outlet using the approved power adapter and confirm the connector is fully seated in the pump power adapter port.

steps.title: Verify the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: If the pump does not appear to charge, test the outlet with another approved device or outlet tester and move the pump to a known-good outlet if needed.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Adapter and Cord
steps.instructions: Check for loose connection, damaged cord, bent plug, strain relief damage, intermittent charging when moved, or an incorrect adapter. Reseat the connector fully.

steps.title: Allow Adequate Charging Time
steps.instructions: Leave the pump plugged into wall power for at least 12 hours to fully charge the battery before judging battery performance.

steps.title: Recheck Operation After Charging
steps.instructions: After charging, briefly unplug the pump and confirm whether it operates normally on battery without immediately displaying the Low Battery Notice, then reconnect to wall power.

steps.title: Isolate Adapter Versus Pump or Battery Issue
steps.instructions: If available, test with a known-good compatible adapter and test the reported adapter on another compatible Kangaroo OMNI if facility policy allows.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after proper connection to wall power and the pump charges normally, complete basic functional verification, document findings, and return the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump displayed Low Battery Notice during feeding or flushing. Staff reported pump was low on battery with approximately 30 minutes of battery life remaining.
CCR.Cause: Pump battery was depleted. Power adapter connection and wall outlet were checked. Pump accepted wall power after adapter was fully seated and was placed on charge.
CCR.Resolution: Connected pump to known-good wall outlet, verified power adapter was fully inserted into pump power adapter port, and charged pump. Alarm cleared and pump operation verified. Returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Low Battery Caution
description: Troubleshooting Low Battery Caution alarms on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump when battery life is critically low but feeding or flushing may continue.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is actively feeding or flushing a patient, do not ignore the alarm. Confirm clinical staff is aware that approximately 15 minutes of battery life remains. Connect the pump to AC power immediately or replace it with a known-good feeding pump if AC power is not available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Check the pump display and confirm the alarm is specifically Low Battery Caution. Ask staff when the alarm appeared and confirm whether the pump is still delivering fluid.

steps.title: Connect the Pump to Wall Power
steps.instructions: Plug the Kangaroo OMNI into a known-working wall outlet using the approved power adapter. Confirm the adapter is connected to the pump’s power adapter port and allow charging to begin.

steps.title: Confirm the Outlet Is Working
steps.instructions: If the pump does not appear to recognize AC power, test the wall outlet with another approved device or move the pump to a known-good outlet. Avoid loose outlets, damaged power strips, or questionable extension cords.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Adapter Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the power adapter plug is fully inserted into the pump’s power adapter port. Check for a loose connection, partially inserted plug, bent adapter tip, damaged power cord, connector strain, or contamination in the power port.

steps.title: Check the Power Adapter
steps.instructions: Inspect the power adapter for visible damage, overheating, frayed cable sections, cracked housing, or intermittent connection. If available, try a known-good compatible power adapter.

steps.title: Charge the Pump Fully
steps.instructions: Leave the pump connected to wall power for at least 12 hours to fully charge the battery. Do not assume the battery is restored after only a short charging period.

steps.title: Verify Battery Operation After Charging
steps.instructions: After the charge period, confirm the pump powers normally and operates without immediately returning to Low Battery Caution. If facility procedure allows, briefly verify battery operation off AC power after the full charge.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after connecting to AC power, confirming the adapter connection, and completing the charge cycle, return the pump to service after basic functional verification. Document the issue as a low-battery condition corrected by charging or power connection correction.

CCR.Complaint: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump displayed Low Battery Caution alarm during feeding. Staff reported pump was still delivering fluid but battery warning indicated low remaining runtime.
CCR.Cause: Pump battery was critically low. Power adapter connection and wall outlet were checked. Adapter plug was confirmed fully seated in the pump power adapter port. No external power damage noted.
CCR.Resolution: Pump was connected to a known-good wall outlet and charged for at least 12 hours. Alarm cleared after charging. Pump passed basic power and operational verification and was returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Feed Error
description: Troubleshooting the Kangaroo OMNI Feed Error alarm caused by empty feed bags, air bubbles, clogs, kinks, foam, cassette seating issues, or sensor-region contamination.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Notify clinical staff before troubleshooting if the pump is actively delivering feeding or flush. Do not prime while connected to the patient. Disconnect the patient before priming or clearing air. Use a known-good pump if feeding must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Feed Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Feed Error. Ask when the alarm occurred and observe whether the pump stops feeding, continues alarming after reset, or alarms again after priming.

steps.title: Check Whether the Feed Bag Is Empty
steps.instructions: Inspect the feed bag for remaining formula or fluid. If empty, have clinical staff refill or replace the feeding solution, press Continue, and prime the set before restarting feeding.

steps.title: Check for Air Bubbles in the Tubing
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing between the feed bag and pump cassette for visible air gaps or bubbles. Disconnect the patient before priming and prime the set until the line is properly filled.

steps.title: Check for Kinks, Clamps, or Tubing Restrictions
steps.instructions: Trace tubing from the feed bag to the pump. Correct kinks, clamps, twists, pinches, or compression from bed rails, poles, or other equipment.

steps.title: Check for Clogs or Thick Formula Buildup
steps.instructions: Inspect for thickened formula, sediment, dried feeding solution, or visible blockage. Prime the line after disconnecting the patient or replace the feeding set if the blockage cannot be cleared safely.

steps.title: Check for Excessive Foam
steps.instructions: Inspect the feed bag and tubing for excessive foam. Allow formula to settle if clinically appropriate, re-prime the tubing, and verify the bag and set are loaded without trapped air.

steps.title: Reload the Feeding Set Cassette
steps.instructions: Pause the pump, remove the cassette, inspect for improper seating, residue, damage, or contamination, then reload the cassette correctly and restart according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Occlusion Sensor Region
steps.instructions: With the cassette removed, inspect the occlusion sensor region for formula residue, moisture, dried feeding solution, or debris. Clean and dry the area according to facility-approved cleaning practice.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the bag contains formula, tubing is clear, air has been primed out, and the cassette area is clean, replace the feeding set with a compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set and prime before use.

steps.title: Test With a Different Pump if Available
steps.instructions: Move the patient to a known-good pump if feeding must continue. If the alarm follows the feeding set, suspect the set or fluid path. If the alarm remains only with the original pump, suspect pump-side failure.

steps.title: Recognize Alarm Detection Timing
steps.instructions: Feed Error detection may take up to 40 minutes at very low rates such as 1 mL/hr and may occur within about 5 minutes at rates above 50 mL/hr.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after correcting the external cause, verify stable feeding operation before returning the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported with Feed Error alarm during feeding. Staff stated pump alarmed with formula still in the bag and feeding delivery stopped.
CCR.Cause: Found air bubbles and partial tubing restriction between feed bag and pump cassette. Feed Error cleared after tubing was corrected and feeding set was primed with patient disconnected.
CCR.Resolution: Removed air from feeding set, corrected tubing path, reloaded cassette, and verified pump operated without Feed Error returning. Device returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Flush Error
description: Troubleshooting guide for Kangaroo OMNI Flush Error alarms caused by an empty flush bag, kinked tubing, clog, cassette issue, improper priming, or flush occlusion condition.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not repeatedly clear the Flush Error while connected to a patient. Confirm clinical staff is aware, pause use as appropriate, and move the patient to a known-good feeding pump if continued therapy is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Flush Error. Ask when the alarm occurs and whether feeding still works or both feed and flush functions are affected.

steps.title: Understand What the Alarm Means
steps.instructions: Treat the alarm as a possible flush-path restriction or empty-source condition. Common causes include an empty flush bag, clogged or kinked flush tubing, poor priming, cassette loading issues, or contamination near the occlusion sensor region.

steps.title: Check the Flush Bag
steps.instructions: Inspect the flush bag and confirm it contains enough fluid. Refill or replace the bag according to clinical policy and press Continue after correcting the condition.

steps.title: Inspect the Flush Tubing Path
steps.instructions: Trace tubing from the flush bag to the pump. Check for kinks, closed clamps, pinched tubing, visible clogging, dried residue, or improper routing.

steps.title: Verify Flush Tubing Was Properly Primed
steps.instructions: Confirm the flush tubing is primed and free of air or blockage. Disconnect the patient before priming. When using a feed/flush set, prime the flush tubing before the feed tubing.

steps.title: Check Cassette Seating and Loading
steps.instructions: Stop the pump according to facility procedure, remove and reload the cassette, and confirm the tubing is not twisted, stretched, or pinched.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Occlusion Sensor Region
steps.instructions: Remove the cassette and inspect the occlusion sensor area for moisture, formula, residue, or debris. Clean and dry the region according to facility-approved cleaning practices.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set if Needed
steps.instructions: If the flush bag is full, tubing is open, the line is primed, and the cassette area is clean, replace the feeding set with a compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Setup
steps.instructions: Use a known-good flush bag, compatible feeding set, and properly primed tubing. Run a controlled flush test while disconnected from any patient.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported with Flush Error alarm during programmed flush. Staff stated flush would not complete.
CCR.Cause: Flush bag and tubing path inspected. Flush tubing was found kinked between the flush bag and pump, preventing proper flush delivery.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected tubing position, verified flush bag volume, primed flush tubing with patient disconnected, and confirmed flush completed without alarm. Device returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Rotor Stuck
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Rotor Stuck alarm on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump, including patient safety, rotor cleaning, feeding set checks, and escalation when the alarm persists.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not continue relying on the pump if the Rotor Stuck alarm is active while connected to a patient. Pause feeding or flushing according to clinical policy, notify staff, and transfer therapy to a known-good feeding pump if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Rotor Stuck. Ask when the alarm occurred and whether it happened during startup, after loading the set, during feeding or flushing, after fluid exposure, or intermittently during operation.

steps.title: Understand What the Alarm Means
steps.instructions: Recognize that Rotor Stuck indicates unusual resistance on the rotor. Focus first on contamination, feeding set loading, and rotor movement before assuming internal pump failure.

steps.title: Remove Power Before Cleaning
steps.instructions: Unplug the pump from the electrical outlet before cleaning the rotor area. Do not clean the pump while connected to AC power.

steps.title: Inspect the Rotor Area
steps.instructions: Remove the feeding set and inspect the rotor area for dried formula, sticky residue, medication buildup, contamination, obstruction, or visible damage.

steps.title: Clean the Rotor
steps.instructions: Wipe the rotor with a wet cloth, remove visible residue or contaminants, avoid spraying liquid directly into the pump, and allow the area to dry before reloading the feeding set.

steps.title: Reload the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: Install the feeding set correctly and confirm it is seated properly. Check for pinched, misrouted, pulled, or interfering tubing.

steps.title: Power On and Recheck Operation
steps.instructions: Reconnect power if needed, power on the pump, and test operation under facility-approved conditions. If the alarm clears, complete verification and document the correction.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Feeding Set if Needed
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues, replace the current feeding set with a known-good compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set and check whether the alarm returns.

steps.title: Isolate the Pump
steps.instructions: If the Rotor Stuck alarm continues after cleaning and using a known-good feeding set, power off the pump, remove it from service, and use a different pump for patient care.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported with Rotor Stuck alarm during feeding. Staff stated pump stopped and alarm returned after setup.
CCR.Cause: Rotor area had visible enteral fluid residue or contaminant buildup causing increased rotor resistance. Alarm cleared after cleaning and reloading feeding set.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from patient use for evaluation. Unplugged pump before cleaning. Cleaned rotor with wet cloth, allowed area to dry, reloaded feeding set, and verified operation without Rotor Stuck alarm. Returned pump to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Patient Tube Blocked
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Patient Tube Blocked alarm on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump when feeding solution is not being delivered due to a downstream tubing restriction.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Treat the alarm as an interruption in feeding delivery. Notify clinical staff before disconnecting tubing, clearing a blockage, or restarting feeding. Use a backup feeding pump if ordered feeding cannot be restored quickly and safely.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Patient Tube Blocked. Ask when the alarm occurred and whether feeding stopped or the alarm returned after being cleared.

steps.title: Inspect the Tubing Between the Pump and Patient
steps.instructions: Follow the tubing path from the pump outlet toward the patient. Check for kinks, closed clamps, pinched tubing, sharp bends, formula buildup, or patient connection obstruction.

steps.title: Confirm the Feeding Set Is Properly Positioned
steps.instructions: Verify the cassette is fully seated and that tubing is not stretched, twisted, pinched, or misrouted through the cassette area.

steps.title: Clear the Alarm and Restart Feeding
steps.instructions: After the tubing path has been checked and any restriction has been cleared, press Continue and restart feeding according to clinical procedure.

steps.title: Remove, Inspect, and Reload the Cassette
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues, remove the cassette. Inspect for residue, moisture, debris, tubing seating issues, or contamination near the occlusion sensor region. Clean and dry the occlusion sensor region and reload the cassette.

steps.title: Restart Feeding After Cassette Reload
steps.instructions: Press Continue, restart feeding, and observe the pump for repeat downstream blockage alarms.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues after tubing inspection, cassette reload, and sensor area cleaning, replace and prime a new feeding set according to clinical procedure.

steps.title: Try a Different Pump if Needed
steps.instructions: If the same tubing setup works correctly on another Kangaroo OMNI pump, suspect a pump-related sensing or mechanism issue. If the alarm follows the set, suspect the tubing, cassette, or patient-side line.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported Patient Tube Blocked alarm during feeding. Staff stated feeding stopped and formula was not being delivered to the patient.
CCR.Cause: Downstream tubing between pump and patient was kinked, causing restricted flow and Patient Tube Blocked alarm.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared tubing restriction, verified cassette was seated correctly, pressed Continue, restarted feeding, and confirmed pump operated without recurring alarm. Returned pump to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Cassette Dislodged
description: Troubleshooting Cassette Dislodged alarms caused by improper cassette loading, feeding set issues, contamination, or pump cassette detection failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Kangaroo OMNI is connected to a patient and the cassette alarm prevents feeding or flushing, stop therapy according to clinical policy and provide another verified pump if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump display shows Cassette Dislodged and determine when the alarm occurred, such as after loading, at startup, during feeding, during flushing, or after the pump was moved.

steps.title: Remove the Pump From Active Patient Use Before Reloading
steps.instructions: Pause therapy and follow facility procedure before manipulating the feeding set. Do not reload, prime, or troubleshoot the cassette while connected to the patient unless clinical staff confirms it is safe.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette
steps.instructions: Remove the cassette, reload it fully into the pump, confirm it sits flat and properly aligned, then press Continue and verify whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Check for Simple Loading Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect the cassette area for residue, moisture, formula buildup, or other visible contamination that could prevent proper cassette seating. Clean externally using approved practices and allow the area to dry.

steps.title: Inspect the Feeding Set Cassette
steps.instructions: Check the cassette for cracks, warping, bent plastic, loose components, formula buildup, tubing tension, or improper tubing routing.

steps.title: Replace the Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues after reloading, replace the Kangaroo OMNI feeding set with a new compatible set, load the new cassette, press Continue, and verify whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Check for Pump-Specific Behavior
steps.instructions: Test the suspect pump with a known-good feeding set if appropriate. Determine whether the issue follows the feeding set or remains with the pump.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after reloading or replacing the set, verify normal pump operation during setup, priming, and start of feeding before returning the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported with Cassette Dislodged alarm. Staff stated cassette alarm occurred after loading feeding set and pump would not continue feeding.
CCR.Cause: Cassette was not fully seated in the pump. Alarm cleared after cassette was removed, reloaded, and accepted by the pump.
CCR.Resolution: Reloaded cassette, pressed Continue, verified Cassette Dislodged alarm cleared, and confirmed pump operated normally during setup. Device returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Cassette Error
description: Troubleshooting Cassette Error alarms on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump caused by cassette loading, disposable feeding set irregularities, contamination, or pump-side cassette detection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is connected to a patient and the Cassette Error alarm is active, confirm feeding has stopped or is unreliable. Transfer feeding to another known-good pump if nutrition delivery must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Cassette Error. Ask staff when the alarm occurred, including during startup, after loading the set, shortly after feeding begins, intermittently during feeding, or only with one specific feeding set.

steps.title: Remove the Cassette and Inspect the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: Stop the pump and remove the cassette. Inspect the cassette and tubing for improper seating, warped housing, pinched tubing, kinks, crushed tubing, twisted tubing, debris, residue, or visible fluid contamination.

steps.title: Correct Any Obvious Cassette or Tubing Irregularities
steps.instructions: Straighten tubing, remove twists or kinks, and confirm the cassette is not damaged, contaminated, warped, crushed, or improperly positioned. Replace the feeding set if the cassette or tubing appears damaged.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette Properly
steps.instructions: Reload the cassette into the pump, confirm it is fully seated and aligned, close the loading mechanism completely, press Continue if prompted, and restart feeding using an appropriate test setup.

steps.title: Check for External Contamination or Moisture
steps.instructions: Inspect the cassette loading area for formula, water, residue, or debris. Clean and dry the external cassette area according to facility-approved cleaning practices.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the Cassette Error returns after reloading, replace the feeding set with a new compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set. Prime and load the new set correctly before retesting.

steps.title: Test Pump Operation With the New Set
steps.instructions: Run the pump long enough to determine whether the Cassette Error returns. Consider the programmed flow rate when evaluating recurrence because detection time may be longer at lower rates.

steps.title: Isolate Device-Related Failure
steps.instructions: If the Cassette Error continues after the cassette has been inspected, reloaded, and replaced, remove the pump from use and use a different Kangaroo OMNI pump for patient feeding.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported with Cassette Error alarm during feeding. Staff stated pump alarmed and feeding delivery was interrupted.
CCR.Cause: Cassette removed and inspected. Tubing/cassette irregularity suspected. Alarm cleared after cassette was reloaded and feeding set was replaced.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced feeding set, reloaded cassette, primed, and verified pump operation without recurring Cassette Error. Device returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Valve Error
description: Troubleshooting Valve Error alarms on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump caused by valve actuator resistance, blockage, contamination, misorientation, cassette seating problems, or feeding set issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm feeding has stopped, notify clinical staff, and move the patient to another verified feeding pump if feeding must continue. Do not troubleshoot while the patient depends on this pump for uninterrupted nutrition or hydration.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump display shows Valve Error. Note whether the alarm occurs after cassette loading, after pressing Start or Continue, during feeding, after pump movement, or only with one feeding set.

steps.title: Translate the Alarm Into the Real-World Condition
steps.instructions: Treat the alarm as a valve actuator or cassette interface issue. Check for cassette seating problems, valve binding, debris, dried formula, contamination, actuator misorientation, damaged set material, or a pump-side valve mechanism problem.

steps.title: Remove the Cassette
steps.instructions: Stop the pump, remove the cassette, and inspect the cassette and valve interface area for improper seating, bent set material, dried feeding solution, sticky residue, visible obstruction, or fluid contamination.

steps.title: Inspect the Valve Actuator
steps.instructions: Check the valve actuator for blockage, residue, abnormal position, sticking, tilting, obstruction, or contamination. Do not force the actuator or perform internal disassembly.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Valve Actuator Area
steps.instructions: Clean the valve actuator area using approved facility cleaning practices. Remove dried formula or contamination and allow the area to fully dry before reloading the cassette.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette Correctly
steps.instructions: Reload the cassette, confirm it is fully seated and aligned, press Continue if prompted, and attempt to restart feeding using a safe test setup or approved clinical workflow.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the Valve Error returns, replace the feeding set with a new compatible set. Prime and load the new set correctly, disconnecting from the patient before priming if required.

steps.title: Try a Different Kangaroo OMNI Pump
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues after cleaning, drying, reloading, and replacing the feeding set, use a different Kangaroo OMNI pump for patient feeding and remove the original pump from service.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Verify that the Valve Error does not return, the cassette remains seated correctly, feeding starts normally, controls respond normally, and no unusual sounds or binding are observed before returning the device to service.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported Valve Error alarm. Staff stated pump stopped feeding and alarm returned after attempting to continue.
CCR.Cause: Valve actuator area found with dried feeding solution and possible cassette binding. Alarm cleared after cassette removal, actuator cleaning/drying, and proper cassette reload.
CCR.Resolution: Removed cassette, inspected valve actuator for obstruction and orientation, cleaned and dried valve actuator area, reloaded cassette, and verified pump resumed operation without Valve Error alarm. Device returned to service after functional check.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Sensor Error 1
description: Troubleshooting Sensor Error 1 on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump caused by occlusion sensor contamination, cassette tubing debris, feeding set issues, moisture, or sensor degradation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Kangaroo OMNI is connected to a patient, do not troubleshoot the alarm while feeding is actively needed. Pause therapy according to facility procedure and provide another feeding pump if nutrition or flush delivery must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that the display shows Sensor Error 1. Ask when the alarm occurred, including startup, feeding, cassette loading, cassette removal, cassette replacement, or intermittent use. Note whether feeding stopped, whether Continue was available, and whether the alarm returned after restart.

steps.title: Remove the Cassette
steps.instructions: Remove the feeding set cassette from the pump. Inspect the cassette area and tubing inside the cassette for lint, debris, dried formula, moisture, contamination, improper seating, or damaged tubing.

steps.title: Inspect the Occlusion Sensor Region
steps.instructions: Check the occlusion sensor region for residue, fluid, buildup, or debris. Do not use sharp objects, picks, metal tools, or anything that could damage the sensor surface. Use approved cleaning methods and a soft bristle brush if necessary.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Area
steps.instructions: Clean the cassette loading area and occlusion sensor region. Allow the area to fully dry before reloading the cassette. Confirm there is no moisture or cleaning residue.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette Correctly
steps.instructions: Reload the cassette into the pump. Confirm it is fully seated and aligned. Press Continue if prompted, then restart feeding using a safe test setup or per clinical workflow.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If Sensor Error 1 returns, replace the Kangaroo OMNI feeding set with a known-good compatible set. Reload the new cassette and restart feeding.

steps.title: Isolate Pump vs. Disposable Set
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears with a new feeding set, document the issue as feeding set or cassette-related. If the alarm continues with a known-good feeding set, treat the issue as pump-related and remove the device from service.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported Sensor Error 1 during feeding. Staff reported feeding stopped and alarm returned after restart.
CCR.Cause: Occlusion sensor region and cassette tubing inspected. Debris or contamination was found in the cassette sensor area, causing abnormal occlusion detection sensor behavior.
CCR.Resolution: Removed cassette, cleaned and dried occlusion sensor region using non-sharp cleaning method, inspected tubing inside cassette, reloaded cassette, pressed Continue, and verified feeding resumed without Sensor Error 1. Pump returned to service.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Sensor Error 2
description: Troubleshooting SENSOR ERROR 2 on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump caused by force sensor detection, cassette loading issues, sensor contamination, feeding set problems, or sensor degradation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the pump is connected to a patient and the alarm prevents feeding, stop feeding according to clinical procedure and move the patient to another verified feeding pump if nutrition delivery must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the pump and confirm the exact alarm displayed is SENSOR ERROR 2. Document whether it occurs on power-up, after cassette removal, after cassette loading, intermittently during setup, or only with one feeding set.

steps.title: Remove the Feeding Set Cassette
steps.instructions: Remove the cassette and inspect the cassette bay for improper seating, formula residue, moisture, dried feeding solution, debris, or damaged cassette components.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Occlusion Sensor Region
steps.instructions: Clean the occlusion sensor region using facility-approved procedure. Make sure the area is fully dry before reinstalling the feeding set. Do not scrape or press hard on the sensor area.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette Correctly
steps.instructions: Reload the cassette carefully, confirm it is seated correctly, press Continue if prompted, and attempt to restart feeding using an appropriate setup.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues, install a new compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set and confirm the replacement cassette is not damaged, contaminated, or expired.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the cassette bay and sensor area for cracked plastic, sticky residue, liquid intrusion, bent cassette contact areas, drop damage, or difficulty inserting and removing the cassette.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Turn the pump off, wait briefly, and power it back on without a cassette installed. If SENSOR ERROR 2 appears immediately with no cassette or obstruction present, remove the pump from service.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If cleaning, drying, cassette reseating, or feeding set replacement clears the alarm, run a basic operational check before returning the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported SENSOR ERROR 2 alarm during startup or cassette loading and prevented normal feeding setup.
CCR.Cause: Occlusion and force sensor region inspected. Cassette area cleaned and dried. Alarm persisted after cassette reload and replacement feeding set, indicating likely internal force sensor degradation or sensor detection failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed cassette, cleaned and dried sensor region, reloaded cassette, pressed Continue, and tested with replacement feeding set. SENSOR ERROR 2 remained. Pump removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation. Alternate pump placed into use.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Sensor Error 3
description: Troubleshooting Sensor Error 3 on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump when force sensor degradation, cassette contact problems, residue, moisture, or feeding set issues may prevent normal feeding operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Kangaroo OMNI is connected to a patient and Sensor Error 3 prevents feeding, do not continue relying on that pump. Pause therapy according to clinical policy and transfer feeding to another verified feeding pump if feeding must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the pump on and confirm the displayed alarm is Sensor Error 3. Observe whether the alarm occurs at startup, after cassette removal, during cassette loading, or intermittently after handling the cassette area.

steps.title: Remove the Feeding Set Cassette
steps.instructions: Remove the cassette and inspect the cassette area for improper seating, distorted cassette surfaces, formula residue, moisture, sticky buildup, or debris around the sensor contact area.

steps.title: Clean and Dry the Occlusion / Force Sensor Region
steps.instructions: Clean the occlusion sensor and cassette contact region using facility-approved cleaning methods. Make sure the area is fully dry before reloading the cassette. Do not scrape, pry, or press aggressively on the sensor area.

steps.title: Reload the Cassette Correctly
steps.instructions: Install the cassette again and make sure it is fully seated in the cassette loading area. Press Continue if prompted, then restart the feeding setup and confirm whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Replace the Feeding Set
steps.instructions: If Sensor Error 3 continues after cleaning and reloading the cassette, remove the feeding set and install a new compatible Kangaroo OMNI feeding set to rule out cassette or set-related causes.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the pump around the cassette and sensor area for cracked plastic, sticky formula residue, dried enteral nutrition, fluid intrusion, latch damage, or abnormal sensor positioning.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Retest
steps.instructions: Power the pump off, wait briefly, then power it back on without a cassette installed. Observe whether Sensor Error 3 appears immediately during startup or after cassette removal.

steps.title: Isolate the Cause
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after cleaning, the issue was likely residue, moisture, or obstruction. If it clears after replacing the feeding set, the issue was likely cassette-related. If it continues with a clean sensor area and new feeding set, suspect internal force sensor degradation or pump hardware failure.

CCR.Complaint: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported Sensor Error 3 during power-up or cassette handling and prevented normal feeding operation.
CCR.Cause: Occlusion/force sensor region inspected and cleaned. Feeding set cassette was reloaded and replaced with a known-good set. Alarm persisted, indicating likely internal force sensor degradation or pump sensor hardware failure.
CCR.Resolution: Pump removed from patient use, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation/repair. Clinical area advised to use a different Kangaroo OMNI pump.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: Dead Battery Error
description: Troubleshooting a Dead Battery Error on the Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump when battery failure is imminent and feeding or flushing may stop due to battery loss.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Kangaroo OMNI is actively feeding or flushing and the Dead Battery Error is present, notify clinical staff immediately. Do not allow the pump to continue operating on battery power with this alarm active. If therapy must continue, transfer the feeding setup to a known-good pump or connect the current pump to AC power immediately.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Dead Battery Error or Dead Battery Warning. Ask staff whether the alarm occurred during feeding, during flushing, while unplugged from AC power, shortly after being placed on battery, or while already connected to wall power. Confirm whether the pump stopped delivering fluid or required operation to be restarted.

steps.title: Connect the Pump to Wall Power Immediately
steps.instructions: Plug the Kangaroo OMNI into a known-working wall outlet using the approved power adapter. Confirm the adapter is fully seated in the pump power adapter port. Confirm the outlet is live by using another device or an outlet tester if needed.

steps.title: Restart Pump Operation
steps.instructions: Once AC power is connected, restart pump operation as required. Confirm that the pump powers on normally and allows feeding or flushing to resume. Verify that the programmed therapy settings are correct before returning to clinical use.

steps.title: Check for Charging Response
steps.instructions: Leave the pump connected to AC power and observe whether the battery indicator shows charging activity. Confirm there are no repeated dead battery, low battery, or power-related alarms while connected.

steps.title: Inspect External Power Components
steps.instructions: Check the power adapter, cord, plug, and pump power port for loose connections, bent or damaged plugs, frayed cords, cracked adapter housing, contamination in the power port, or intermittent power when the cord is moved. Try a known-good Kangaroo OMNI power adapter if available and approved for use.

steps.title: Allow Adequate Charging Time
steps.instructions: If the pump powers normally on AC power, allow the battery to charge according to facility practice. Do not return the pump for battery-dependent use until charging and battery operation are verified.

steps.title: Test Battery Operation After Charging
steps.instructions: After sufficient charge time, disconnect AC power briefly in a controlled bench or non-patient setting. Confirm the pump remains powered without immediately alarming for Dead Battery Error. Reconnect AC power after the test.

steps.title: Identify Battery Pack Failure
steps.instructions: If AC power is confirmed good but the pump will not charge, immediately returns to dead battery, or cannot maintain battery operation, suspect a failed battery pack. Remove the pump from clinical use and route it for qualified battery replacement or bench evaluation.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the pump charges normally, restarts correctly, and passes battery operation verification, document the findings and return the pump to service. Confirm the final status with clinical staff if the pump was removed from the floor.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump reported Dead Battery Error during use. Staff stated pump was near battery loss and feeding or flushing operation had to be restarted.
CCR.Cause: Battery was fully depleted or battery pack failed to recover charge after confirmed AC power connection. External power source and adapter connection were checked.
CCR.Resolution: Connected pump to known-good wall outlet, verified power adapter seating, restarted operation, and monitored charging response. Pump continued to show dead battery condition and was removed from service for battery replacement or bench evaluation.

title: Cardinal Health Kangaroo OMNI Feeding Pump - Alarm: System Error
description: Troubleshooting Kangaroo OMNI System Error alarms that may stop pump operation, interrupt feeding or flushing, or indicate a device-level system fault.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Kangaroo OMNI is actively feeding or flushing and a System Error occurs, do not continue relying on the pump. Stop use of the affected pump and ensure the patient’s feeding or flushing therapy is managed by clinical staff using an appropriate backup pump if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump display shows System Error. Document whether it occurs during startup, feeding, flushing, cassette loading, or intermittent use. Note whether the pump stops delivery, will not restart, powers off unexpectedly, repeats after restart, or displays a three-number code.

steps.title: Follow the On-Screen Restart Instruction
steps.instructions: Follow the restart instruction shown on the display. Power the pump off as directed, then turn it back on. Confirm whether the System Error clears.

steps.title: Confirm Whether the Alarm Returns
steps.instructions: After restart, observe the pump through normal startup. If clinically appropriate, test with a proper feeding set and a non-patient test setup. Continue functional verification before returning the pump to service if the alarm does not return.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Inspect the pump exterior for visible damage, fluid intrusion, contamination, cracked housing, damaged buttons, or signs of impact. Confirm the pump is physically intact, clean, and dry.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Adapter Connection
steps.instructions: Connect the Kangaroo OMNI to a known-good wall outlet using the correct power adapter. Confirm the adapter is fully inserted into the pump power port and avoid relying on battery power during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Remove Accessories and Reload Only What Is Needed
steps.instructions: Remove the feeding set or cassette if installed. Inspect for cassette misloading, damage, fluid leakage, or contamination around the cassette loading area. Reload a clean, known-good Kangaroo OMNI feeding set if testing is needed.

steps.title: Check for Repeatability
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump again and observe whether the System Error returns. Record the exact alarm name, three-number code, when it appears, whether it occurs on AC power, and whether it occurs with or without a feeding set installed.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the System Error clears after restart and does not return during functional verification, document that the alarm was not reproducible after restart and that external checks passed. Return the pump to service only after confirming normal operation.

CCR.Complaint: Kangaroo OMNI feeding pump displayed System Error during use. Pump stopped normal operation and required restart. Three-number code was present on screen.
CCR.Cause: System Error persisted after power cycle. AC power, external condition, cassette area, and feeding set setup were checked. Findings indicate likely internal device-level system fault.
CCR.Resolution: Recorded three-number System Error code, powered pump off, removed device from patient use, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair or manufacturer support evaluation.
